title: Unprotected approveToken allows infinite token approvals to potentially compromised addresses
reason: Not a standalone vault bug. After constructor the function generally reverts because `safeApprove` forbids non-zero-to-non-zero approvals, and any remaining approval risk depends on trusted external protocol/admin compromise rather than permissionless misuse here.

title: Share calculation uses incorrect pool value leading to incorrect deposit amounts
reason: Using the pre-deposit pool value is the standard share-minting formula; the report's same-block unfairness claim is unsupported by this code.

title: Division by zero in getPricePerFullShare when totalSupply is zero
reason: This is a view helper reverting before initialization, not a realistic protocol-level loss, theft, insolvency, or lockup issue.

title: Dynamic Aave address approval in constructor can redirect approvals to attacker-controlled address
reason: This relies on Aave governance/admin compromise or trusted address-provider changes, not a permissionless exploit introduced by the vault itself.

title: Missing return value check on Aave deposit
reason: The declared Aave interface returns no value. A failed deposit would revert rather than silently return false, so there is no unchecked-return vulnerability here.

title: Rounding errors in withdrawal calculations can cause user fund loss
reason: The `add(1)` intentionally over-redeems by at most dust to satisfy the requested withdrawal; it does not by itself create realistic protocol-level loss.

title: Recommend function uses external call that can be manipulated in single transaction
reason: Too speculative as written. The report does not establish how the APR source is derived or that it is flash-loan manipulable in a way that creates concrete vault loss.

title: Missing event emissions for critical ownership functions
reason: Operational transparency issue only; not a reportable protocol-level vulnerability under the requested impact standard.

title: Inconsistent balanceOf implementations may cause confusion
reason: Integration ergonomics issue, not a security vulnerability.
